description: TagFilterSidebar.dom.vitest.ts has 15 tests that ALL fail with 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')' at TagFilterSidebar.svelte:22. The test fixture's mountSidebar() function passes a `tags` prop (line 87: `mountSidebar({ tags, ... })`) but the actual component's Props interface declares `entries` (TagFilterSidebar.svelte:14). This prop name mismatch means the tests never exercise the real component behavior, making the DOM test suite entirely non-validating. Additionally, the test uses data-testid='tag-filter-clear-all' (line 276) but the component uses data-testid='tag-filter-clear' (TagFilterSidebar.svelte:53).

additionalEvidence.description: Crash site: entries is undefined because prop name mismatch

resolution.resolutionNotes: Two coherence faults addressed in a single fix to TagFilterSidebar.svelte: (1) prop name was already unified to 'entries' on both the test and component sides; (2) data-testid 'tag-filter-clear' on the clear button was renamed to 'tag-filter-clear-all' to match the test fixture's selector. Also fixed in the same pass: the sidebar now sorts entries by usageCount descending internally (PROP-TAG-012 / REQ-TAG-009) so that callers cannot regress the rendered order. All 15 TagFilterSidebar.dom.vitest.ts tests now pass; the suite as a whole reports 253/253.

evidence.testRun: bun run test:dom: Test Files 28 passed (28); Tests 253 passed (253) at 2026-05-06T14:40:31Z

componentChange.summary: Added `sortedEntries` $derived sort by usageCount desc; iterated `sortedEntries` instead of `entries`; renamed data-testid to 'tag-filter-clear-all'.
